summary: The gold standard for measuring anatomical dead space is the xenon dilution technique, as it provides an absolutely accurate, non-assumption-based quantification of dead space volume compared to estimated or indirectly derived methods such as those using nitrogren washout or spirometric calculations. This makes xenon dilution superior both technically and scientifically despite being less accessible in routine clinical use.
